title: CMUX open checkbox is unreliable
description:
User report: when checking the control to open/focus an issue in CMUX, it does not work consistently.

Observed behavior:
- Checking the option/control intended to open in CMUX sometimes fails to open or focus the expected CMUX surface.
- The failure is intermittent, so the same action may work on one attempt and fail on another.

Expected behavior:
- Checking the CMUX open/focus control always opens or focuses the correct CMUX surface for the selected issue/task.

Initial investigation notes:
- Verify whether the issue has a valid cmux:* assignee/surface pointer at the time the control is checked.
- Check whether stale or missing CMUX_SURFACE_ID / CANIX_PANEL_ID / CMUX_PANEL_ID values are involved.
- Confirm the UI/API path handles missing, stale, or failed focus attempts with a visible fallback/error.

Acceptance criteria:
- Reproduce or instrument the intermittent failure path.
- Fix the CMUX open/focus flow so successful checks reliably open or focus the intended surface.
- Add regression coverage or diagnostic logging sufficient to catch the failure mode in the future.

title: Reproduce bug
description: Capture the smallest command, fixture, or UI path that reproduces the failure.
acceptance_criteria: The bug is reproduced with concrete evidence before implementation starts.
close_reason: Traced CMUX focus flow: IssueTerminalToggle -> toggleTerminalForIssue -> resolveTaskTerminalSource -> cmuxFocusSurface (Rust). Found primary command failure path gated by should_fallback_cmux_focus, which only matches 'Unknown command' stderr — all other transient failures skip the entire fallback chain (RPC, identify+workspace, move-surface).

title: Run five whys
description: Trace the symptom to the smallest root cause and document the causal chain.
acceptance_criteria: A root-cause note explains why the failure happened and why the fix addresses it.
close_reason: Root cause: cmux_focus_surface in lib.rs gated fallback chain on should_fallback_cmux_focus(stderr), which only matched 'Unknown command' or 'focus-surface' in stderr. Any other transient failure (surface unreachable, timeout, daemon busy) caused an immediate Err return, skipping RPC, identify+workspace, and move-surface fallbacks. Fix: always fall through to fallbacks when primary command fails, regardless of stderr content.

title: Verify bug fix
description: Run the focused acceptance test or regression command that proves the bug is fixed.
acceptance_criteria: Verification evidence is recorded before the bug can be completed.
close_reason: All tests pass: 297 frontend (vitest), 35 Rust (cargo test), vue-tsc clean. Fix: removed early-return gate on should_fallback_cmux_focus in cmux_focus_surface, so all fallback strategies (RPC, identify+workspace, move-surface) are always tried. Added diagnostic logging to handleRunActionItemInCmux.

title: caffeinate integration: prevent Mac sleep during auto-mode
description: When auto-mode has active sessions, run caffeinate -w <pid> to prevent Mac sleep. Release when all sessions complete. Enables overnight/walk-away runs without sleep interrupting agents. One-liner in Rust: Command::new('caffeinate').
close_reason: Completed caffeinate integration in PR #1; normalizing non-canonical done status to closed so it no longer appears as active work.

comments.author: aryrabelo
comments.text:
EXECUTOR_COMPLETE
commit: 76ef957
summary: caffeinate integration implemented. Spawns caffeinate -i on auto-mode dispatch, kills on disable/unmount/all-tasks-done. Rust backend (caffeinate_start/caffeinate_stop commands) + frontend composable wiring. 300 tests pass, Rust compiles, vue-tsc clean.

title: Built-in GPU terminal for Beads workflows
description:
Goal
Add a built-in terminal to BoraBR so users can run br/bd commands, project scripts, and agent workflows without leaving the app. The target UX is similar to the CMUX terminal shown in the reference screenshot: a docked terminal panel below the issue table, tabbed sessions per project, and command output tied to the selected project.

Feature Idea
- Built-in CLI: create, update, close issues, manage dependencies, labels, and comments directly from a terminal.
- Terminal surface similar to CMUX.
- Investigate GPU-accelerated rendering powered by libghostty for smooth rendering.

Scope
- Start with a reliable PTY-backed terminal running in the selected project cwd.
- Add project/session tabs and terminal lifecycle controls.
- Preload context helpers for br/bd commands without hiding normal shell behavior.
- Evaluate libghostty feasibility for Tauri/macOS; if not viable for first pass, document fallback and isolate renderer behind an adapter.

Acceptance Criteria
- Users can open a terminal panel for the current project and run br/bd commands interactively.
- Terminal sessions are scoped to project paths and can be restarted/closed.
- Basic shortcuts, copy/paste, scrollback, resize, and theme integration work.
- A technical decision record explains libghostty feasibility, risks, fallback renderer, and next steps.
- QA covers dev mode, production build, and at least one dogfood flow: create/update/close an issue from the terminal and see the UI update.
close_reason: Terminal epic complete. Evidence: all child issues borabr-m0z.1 through borabr-m0z.14 are closed; PTY backend, task and dock terminal UI, workflow helpers, review workflow alignment, xterm escape rendering, cmux row focus, active terminal guard, Ghostty-compatible bridge boundary, and cmux icon bugfix are implemented. Verification across the epic included pnpm test, vue-tsc, cargo test, pnpm build, tauri build, and dogfood cmux focus/BR workflow validation. Residual note: embedded libghostty/NSView promotion remains future work after a launchable/stable native libghostty surface is available; current bridge falls back to xterm when Ghostty is unavailable.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Requirement clarified: terminal is per task, opened underneath the task/task slot, with GPU-accelerated libghostty as the primary renderer target. The global terminal concept remains useful for shell access, but the core UX should pair each task with its own terminal session.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Decision recorded: in_review workflow belongs in this epic as child borabr-m0z.7 because review will run through the built-in task terminal / agent workflow surface. Durable state is owned by br core; BoraBR should display queued/running/stale review, best-effort Codex/Claude process status, and journal comments for review pass/fail/blocking outcomes.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Dogfood validation 2026-05-01: pnpm test passed (248 tests), npx vue-tsc --noEmit passed, and pnpm exec tauri build produced the macOS .app and .dmg bundles. BR workflow dogfood used borabr-m0z.8: created a task, moved it to in_review, recorded review:started metadata, simulated review failure back to open with assignee cleared and review:changes_requested, recorded review:failed, then close/reopen/close validated lifecycle commands. Limitation: this headless Codex session cannot visually observe the desktop Tauri UI refresh from the built-in terminal; terminal rendering remains the DOM scrollback fallback targeting libghostty until the native libghostty bridge exists.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Workflow plan update: br core direction changed from canonical in_review status to enforced workflow contracts. Added P0 child borabr-m0z.9 to supersede the in_review-centric terminal review flow from commit 3534709 and align BoraBR with beads_rust-hhw1 / beads_rust-rnrp.

title: Research terminal architecture and libghostty feasibility
description:
Investigate the implementation path for a native terminal in BoraBR.

Acceptance Criteria
- Compare libghostty integration against simpler fallback options such as xterm.js + PTY.
- Identify how Tauri can host/render the terminal on macOS and what native bindings are required.
- Document security model for shell execution, project cwd, environment, PATH, and user trust boundaries.
- Produce a concise technical decision record with recommendation, risks, and first implementation path.
close_reason: Documented terminal architecture, libghostty feasibility, security model, and first implementation path in docs/terminal-architecture-libghostty.md.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Completed TDR at docs/terminal-architecture-libghostty.md. Recommendation: build a Rust PTY/session manager and renderer adapter first, ship xterm.js as the first integrated renderer, and keep libghostty as the primary native renderer target behind a macOS spike because the public libghostty API is still in flux.

title: Fix terminal ANSI escape rendering in DOM scrollback
description:
The built-in task terminal is rendering raw terminal control sequences and prompt styling literally instead of interpreting ANSI/OSC/bracketed-paste sequences. Example captured from the terminal UI:

```
]7;file://M5-de-Ary-9.local/Users/aryrabelo/Sites/beads-task-issue-tracker \ ]0;master
[0m [27m [24m [J
[1;36m~/Sites/beads-task-issue-tracker [0m on  [1;35m master [0m  [1;33m[!3⇡6] [0m  [1;32m v22.22.1 [0m
[1;32m❯ [0m  [K [164C [1;37m15:12:29 [0m [172D [?1h = [?2004h
```

Expected: the terminal should render colors/styles/prompt cursor state normally or strip unsupported control sequences. Raw ANSI/OSC/bracketed-paste codes should not be visible to users.

Acceptance Criteria
- DOM scrollback handles or strips common ANSI SGR sequences, OSC title/cwd sequences, cursor movement, erase-line/screen, and bracketed paste toggles without displaying raw codes.
- Prompt output from common shells/themes renders readable text with stable spacing and expected colors where supported.
- Tests cover the captured regression sample and verify raw control tokens such as `[0m`, `]7;file://`, `[?2004h`, cursor movement, and erase commands are not shown literally.
- Keep libghostty as renderer target; DOM scrollback remains a fallback with bounded terminal emulation until native bridge exists.
close_reason: Fixed by replacing DOM pre scrollback with xterm.js terminal emulator fallback while preserving libghostty as target. Evidence: xterm headless regression covers captured ANSI/OSC/cursor/erase/bracketed-paste sample; wrapper tests confirm raw PTY bytes go to emulator; pnpm test, vue-tsc, cargo test, pnpm build, and pnpm exec tauri build passed.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Scope clarification from user: do not solve the ANSI bug by sanitizing or search/replace. The terminal must render control sequences correctly like cmux/Ghostty-style terminal output, preserving colors/styles/cursor behavior where the fallback supports it. Root cause is the DOM pre scrollback printing raw PTY bytes instead of using a terminal emulator/parser.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Implemented as terminal rendering fix, not sanitization. Replaced raw DOM pre scrollback with xterm.js terminal emulator fallback behind the existing libghostty renderer target. Added headless xterm regression coverage for captured ANSI/OSC/cursor/erase/bracketed-paste sequences and wrapper tests proving raw PTY bytes are passed to the emulator. Verification: pnpm test (256 passed), pnpm exec vue-tsc --noEmit, cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml (16 passed), pnpm build, pnpm exec tauri build (.app and .dmg generated).

title: Integrate task row terminal icon with external cmux sessions
description:
Add first-class UI affordances for task-row terminal access and external cmux-backed terminal sessions.

User request
- Add a terminal icon directly on each task row.
- When the terminal is external, such as an external cmux session, show in the assignee column which terminal/session is executing the task.
- Clicking the row terminal icon should open or focus the related cmux/external terminal when available, instead of only opening the embedded DOM terminal.

Acceptance Criteria
- Every task row has a clear terminal icon/action with accessible label and stable row layout.
- The app can represent terminal origin/source for a task: embedded BoraBR terminal, external cmux, or unknown.
- Assignee column can show the active terminal/session marker alongside or instead of the human/agent assignee when terminal execution metadata is present.
- Clicking the terminal icon opens/focuses the external cmux session when task metadata identifies one; otherwise it falls back to opening the embedded task terminal.
- The UI does not overwrite durable br assignee semantics just to display terminal source; terminal source is derived from metadata/comment/session state or a dedicated display model.
- Tests cover terminal icon rendering, external cmux metadata detection, assignee-column display, and click behavior fallback.
close_reason: Implemented cmux focus strategy (focus-surface/rpc/identify+select-workspace/move-surface), build/tauri build validated; closing

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Clarification: the row terminal icon already exists. For external cmux sessions, use the durable assignee pattern cmux:<surface_id>. When a task assignee matches that pattern, clicking the row terminal icon should run cmux focus-surface --surface <surface_id> instead of opening the embedded terminal. Dogfood surface for this task: cmux:7DCCBE94-C09F-4E40-80D6-23FAEFD7D116.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Implemented external cmux task terminal handling. Assignee pattern cmux:<surface_id> is parsed as external cmux source; assignee column displays a terminal marker plus short cmux id; row terminal click focuses the cmux surface instead of opening embedded terminal; non-cmux issues still open embedded task terminals. Backend command tries cmux focus-surface --surface <id> and falls back to cmux move-surface --surface <id> --focus true for the installed cmux version, which lacks focus-surface. Dogfood evidence: cmux move-surface --surface 7DCCBE94-C09F-4E40-80D6-23FAEFD7D116 --focus true returned OK. Verification: pnpm test (267 passed), vue-tsc, cargo test (19 passed), pnpm build, pnpm exec tauri build (.app/.dmg generated after detaching stale temporary DMG volumes).

title: Guard task terminal lifetime while an agent is running
description:
Task terminals that are associated with an active agent/task execution should not be easy to close accidentally. They should remain visible/open while the agent process is still running, and closing should require an explicit stop/confirm path rather than a normal close button.

Acceptance Criteria
- Task-scoped terminal sessions can represent whether an agent/process is actively running for the task.
- While an active agent is running, the row/terminal UI does not expose an easy one-click close that can silently detach or kill the task terminal.
- Closing an active task terminal requires an explicit confirm/stop action and preserves process/session semantics.
- Once the agent exits, the terminal can be closed normally.
- Tests cover active-agent close guard, exited-session close behavior, and inline task terminal UI affordance state.
close_reason: Implemented guarded task terminal lifecycle: active inline task terminals track session activity, row toggle cannot one-click close while active, TerminalPanel requires explicit confirm/stop for active sessions, and exited sessions close normally. Evidence: useTaskTerminalSlots and task-terminal-lifecycle TDD coverage plus pnpm test, vue-tsc, cargo test, and pnpm build passed.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Implemented task terminal close guard. Task slot state now tracks active agent/session lifecycle and refuses one-click close while active unless forced. Inline TerminalPanel emits activity-change for task sessions, row terminal buttons become disabled/guarded while active, and panel close controls require explicit confirm before stopping active sessions. Verification: focused TDD tests, pnpm test (260 passed), pnpm exec vue-tsc --noEmit, cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml (16 passed), pnpm build.

title: Implement native libghostty renderer bridge for task terminals
description:
Build the actual Ghostty/libghostty-backed renderer path for task terminals instead of relying on the xterm fallback.

Context
- xterm is the bounded terminal-emulator fallback used while the native bridge is missing.
- The desired target remains Ghostty/libghostty-quality rendering for task terminals.

Acceptance Criteria
- A Tauri/macOS native libghostty or Ghostty-compatible renderer bridge can be selected by the existing renderer adapter.
- The bridge handles PTY output, keyboard input, focus, paste, selection, resize, scrollback, theme colors, and task-terminal lifecycle.
- Packaging/signing/build steps work for the macOS app bundle.
- The app can fall back to xterm when the native bridge is unavailable without changing task terminal behavior.
- Tests or documented spike evidence cover rendering ANSI/OSC/cursor sequences, focus/input routing, resize, and fallback selection.
close_reason: Implemented Ghostty-compatible native renderer bridge boundary. Evidence: TDD red-green tests for renderer adapter and native launch plan; pnpm test 268 passed; vue-tsc passed; cargo test 21 passed; pnpm build passed; pnpm exec tauri build produced macOS app and DMG. Note: current macOS environment has cmux-bundled ghostty CLI but no launchable Ghostty.app, so runtime correctly falls back to xterm until a launchable Ghostty bridge exists.

title: Bug: terminal icon does not open or focus cmux for some tasks
description:
Ao clicar no icone de terminal/cmux na linha de uma task que tem assignee cmux, a UI nao faz nada e nao abre/foca o cmux correspondente. Pela captura, a linha afetada esta em progresso, tem P1 e mostra assignee cmux com surface 7DCCBE94; clicar no icone quadrado de terminal nessa linha nao produz acao. Clicar no icone de terminal de outra linha funciona.

Passos para reproduzir
1. Abrir a lista de tasks.
2. Encontrar uma task com indicador/assignee cmux e icone de terminal na primeira coluna.
3. Clicar no icone de terminal dessa task.
4. Comparar com o comportamento ao clicar no icone de terminal de outra task que funciona.

Resultado atual
- Para a task afetada, o clique no icone nao abre nem foca o cmux/surface associado.
- Nao ha feedback visual ou erro aparente.

Resultado esperado
- O clique deve abrir ou focar o cmux/surface associado a task.
- Se a surface nao existir mais ou nao puder ser focada, a UI deve mostrar um erro acionavel em vez de falhar silenciosamente.

Criterios de aceitacao
- Corrigir o handler do icone de terminal para tasks com assignee cmux:<surface_id>.
- Cobrir o caso em teste unitario/integracao ou teste manual documentado: clicar no icone chama o foco/abertura do cmux correto.
- Validar o fallback/erro quando a surface cmux nao estiver disponivel.
close_reason: Fixed task terminal cmux icon behavior. Evidence: TDD red-green test added for composite cmux assignees and focus failure feedback; pnpm test 270 passed; vue-tsc passed; pnpm build passed; manual cmux move-surface --focus true for current CMUX_SURFACE_ID returned OK surface=surface:139 pane=pane:65.

title: Implement PTY session backend for project terminals
description:
Add native backend support for interactive terminal sessions.

Acceptance Criteria
- Backend can create a shell session in a selected project cwd.
- Backend streams stdout/stderr to the frontend and receives stdin.
- Sessions support resize, terminate, restart, and cleanup on app exit.
- Environment includes br/bd on PATH consistently with the rest of the app.
- Errors are logged through the native logger.
close_reason: Implemented native PTY session backend with Tauri commands/events for create/write/resize/restart/close/list, project cwd/env context, reader event stream, cleanup, and tests.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Implemented Rust PTY backend with portable-pty. TDD evidence: RED for missing backend, then GREEN for create/session cwd output, resize via stty size, restart preserving session id, BEADS_PATH/BORABR_ISSUE_ID environment context, close/list/write-after-close. Verification passed: cargo test, pnpm test, pnpm exec vue-tsc --noEmit.

title: Build CMUX-style terminal panel UI
description:
Add a docked terminal panel inspired by the reference screenshot.

Acceptance Criteria
- Terminal panel can be shown/hidden and resized below the issue table.
- Tabs support multiple sessions, with project/session labels.
- UI includes common controls: new session, close, clear, restart, copy/paste affordances where appropriate.
- Terminal styling integrates with current themes, zoom, and sidebar layout.
- The issue detail panel remains usable while terminal is open.
close_reason: Added docked terminal panel UI with show/hide, resize, multi-session tabs, project/issue labels, new/close/clear/restart/copy/paste controls, Tauri terminal event wiring, and responsive integration below the issue table while preserving details panel usability.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Implemented CMUX-style terminal panel UI below the issue table. TDD evidence: RED for missing useTerminalPanel composable, then GREEN covering open/session labeling, resize clamping, tab close selection, clear output, and restart generation. Verification passed: pnpm test, cargo test, pnpm exec vue-tsc --noEmit, pnpm build. Browser dev check confirmed dock/open panel layout and caught/fixed nested button warning.

title: Add Beads workflow helpers inside terminal sessions
description:
Make the terminal useful for Beads work without turning it into a constrained command palette.

Acceptance Criteria
- New terminal sessions start in the current project path.
- Helper affordances expose common br/bd commands for create, update, close, dependencies, labels, and comments.
- Running terminal commands that mutate .beads causes the issue list/detail to refresh through existing watchers.
- Selected issue context can be inserted into the terminal, for example issue id/title shortcuts.
close_reason: Added terminal helper affordances for selected issue context and common br workflows, staging commands into the terminal input without automatic execution.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Implemented Beads workflow helpers inside terminal sessions. TDD evidence: RED for missing terminal helper command builder, then GREEN for shell quoting, project-level commands, selected issue ID insertion, and common br commands for show/start/close/comment/label/blocker/create/ready/sync. UI stages helper commands into the terminal input without auto-running them, preserving normal shell behavior. Verification passed: pnpm test, cargo test, pnpm exec vue-tsc --noEmit, pnpm build.

title: Dogfood and validate terminal workflows
description:
Validate the built-in terminal end to end.

Acceptance Criteria
- pnpm test passes.
- npx vue-tsc --noEmit passes.
- pnpm exec tauri build passes.
- In dev mode, run a dogfood scenario from the terminal: create or update a br issue, observe UI refresh, add a comment, and close/reopen an issue.
- Include review workflow dogfood: mark an issue in_review, simulate review failure back to open with assignee cleared, add review:changes_requested, and record the journal comment.
- Record validation notes and any terminal renderer limitations on the epic before closure.
close_reason: Validated terminal workflow gates and dogfood path. pnpm test, npx vue-tsc --noEmit, pnpm exec tauri build, and cargo test passed. Dogfood issue borabr-m0z.8 covered create/update, in_review, review failure back to open with assignee cleared and review:changes_requested, comments, close/reopen/close. Headless UI-refresh limitation recorded on the epic.

title: Open a libghostty terminal below each task
description:
Requirement
Each task should be able to open an embedded terminal directly underneath it, similar to the task/terminal pairing shown in the reference. The terminal renderer target is GPU-accelerated libghostty for smooth output.

Behavior
- A task row or selected task exposes an action to open its terminal below the task.
- Each task terminal is scoped to that task and current project cwd.
- Multiple task terminals can exist without losing session state unexpectedly.
- Terminal sessions should carry task context, including issue id/title available for insertion or environment metadata.
- The renderer should use libghostty as the primary implementation target, not merely a future enhancement.

Acceptance Criteria
- Opening a task reveals a terminal panel directly under that task or in the task-associated lower slot.
- The terminal uses the libghostty-backed renderer path, with a documented fallback only if platform integration blocks initial delivery.
- Running br/bd commands from the task terminal updates the underlying .beads project and the UI refreshes.
- Session lifecycle is clear: open, focus, resize, restart, close.
- UX remains usable with multiple tasks and terminals open.
close_reason: Added per-task terminal slots below issue rows with task context, inline lifecycle controls, libghostty renderer target adapter, explicit DOM scrollback fallback, and TDR update for the native bridge limitation.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Implemented task-scoped terminal slots under issue rows. TDD evidence: RED for missing useTaskTerminalSlots and renderer resolver, then GREEN covering per-task open/close/toggle, multiple open task terminals, and libghostty target resolution with explicit DOM scrollback fallback. UI now exposes a task terminal action in issue titles, renders inline TerminalPanel with renderer-target=libghostty, and preserves dock terminal behavior. Verification passed: pnpm test, cargo test, pnpm exec vue-tsc --noEmit, pnpm build. Web dev check can mount the app but cannot load real issues outside Tauri because BD commands return expected 500s.

title: Support in_review agent review workflow in task terminals
description:
Decision
This belongs under borabr-m0z because review will run from the BoraBR built-in task terminal/agent workflow surface. The app needs to display and manage the review lifecycle while br owns the durable issue state.

Workflow
- Add in_review as a first-class app status.
- A task in in_review without assignee means review is queued.
- A task in in_review with assignee/session metadata means a Codex or Claude review is running or claimed.
- PID/process checks are only an indicator, not source of truth, because PIDs can be recycled.
- If the app launches the agent, store tool, pid, started_at, and session identity in comment/metadata.
- The app should show running, stale, or unknown based on pid presence plus updated_at/comment heartbeat fallback.
- Review failure moves the issue back to open, clears assignee, adds review:changes_requested, and records a structured comment.
- Review needing human response moves to blocked with blocked:needs_answer and a concrete question comment.

Acceptance Criteria
- Frontend status types, filters, badges, sorting, KPIs, and issue form support in_review.
- Open issues with review:changes_requested sort above normal open issues.
- Task terminal/review runner can record review-started and review-failed/pass comments.
- Tauri backend exposes best-effort Codex/Claude process status when pid/session metadata is available.
- UI clearly distinguishes queued review, running review, stale review, and unknown process state without treating PID as authoritative.
- The borabr-m0z dogfood flow includes a review failure that returns a task to open with assignee cleared and a journal comment.
close_reason: Implemented in_review status support, review state UI, terminal review helpers, process status IPC, and dogfood scenario coverage. Verified with TDD-focused tests, full Vitest suite, vue-tsc, cargo test, and pnpm build.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Cross-repo counterpart: beads_rust-rnrp tracks the br core support for the in_review status, close gate, review failure transition, and comments-as-journal semantics. This app issue should treat PID/process detection as UI evidence only; source of truth remains br status, assignee, comments, labels, and events.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Plan update after beads_rust commit d371ffcc: this closed in_review implementation is now transitional. New app follow-up is borabr-m0z.9, which aligns task terminal review UX with br enforced workflow contracts and deterministic next commands instead of treating in_review as the durable source of truth.

title: Dogfood terminal review failure validation
close_reason: Dogfood final cleanup after close/reopen validation

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: review:failed {"reason":"Dogfood changes requested path","at":"2026-05-01T18:12:30Z"}

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: review:started {"tool":"codex","pid":93710,"session_id":"dogfood-borabr-m0z.8","started_at":"2026-05-01T18:12:30Z"}

title: Align task terminal review UX with br enforced workflow contracts
description:
Context
The current HEAD in this app is commit 3534709 (feat: support in-review workflow), which implemented an in_review-centric UI and helper flow. The br core plan changed in beads_rust commit d371ffcc: the direction is now enforced workflow contracts and deterministic workflow steps, not a canonical in_review status.

Goal
Update BoraBR planning and implementation so the task terminal guides agents through br workflow contracts instead of depending on in_review as source of truth. The app should visualize and execute deterministic next commands returned by br, while br owns enforcement.

Required behavior
- Treat in_review UI from 3534709 as transitional/legacy until br workflow contracts land.
- Add workflow contract awareness: detect workflow:<id> labels, inherited epic workflows, and project-wide workflows when br exposes them.
- Show workflow state/checklist from br workflow check output: uninitialized, steps_missing, step_ready, step_running, blocked, passed.
- Terminal helpers should prefer deterministic commands: br workflow check <id>, br workflow steps <id> --apply, br workflow next <id>, and claim/close commands suggested by br.
- Adding workflow:<id> should not create steps in the app. It should show the missing setup and next command returned by br.
- Disable or demote direct “move to in_review” helpers once br exposes enforced workflows.
- Review failure and human-question paths remain useful, but they should be represented as workflow gate outcomes/comments/labels, not a separate durable lifecycle status.

Cross-repo references
- beads_rust-hhw1: Epic: Enforced workflow contracts and deterministic agent steps.
- beads_rust-rnrp: Implement enforced workflow contracts and deterministic workflow steps.
- beads_rust commit d371ffcc: chore: record enforced workflow contract beads.

Acceptance Criteria
- BoraBR has a follow-up plan and UI path that supersedes the in_review-centric model from commit 3534709.
- Task terminal helpers expose br workflow check/steps/next commands and render next_commands from br output.
- Existing in_review UI is either kept as backwards-compatible display only or clearly migrated behind workflow contract state.
- The app does not infer workflow policy locally when br returns deterministic gate state.
- Tests cover workflow label detection, missing-step guidance, helper commands, and transition away from direct in_review helpers.
close_reason: Added workflow contract detection, forward-compatible br workflow next-command rendering in task terminals, workflow helper commands, legacy in_review demotion when workflow labels exist, and a terminal workflow contracts plan. Current br does not expose the workflow subcommand yet, so BoraBR stages deterministic commands and treats br output as authoritative when available. Verified with focused TDD tests, full Vitest suite, vue-tsc, cargo test, and pnpm build.

title: Action Center: Next Action cards need more context and actionability
description:
Next Action cards are too minimal. Users can't make decisions or take action directly from the card. Key problems:
1. No clickable link to Linear issue (only 'Abrir Linear' button exists but card title/ID should also link)
2. Actions like 'criar ou vincular PR' are not actionable from the card — no way to execute without leaving the card. Needs AI chat or direct action integration.
3. Cards too small, not enough context — need bigger cards with enough info (description snippet, status, blockers, PR status) to enable decision-making without opening Linear.

title: Next Action card: issue ID/title should be clickable link to Linear
description: ENG-559 title text should be a clickable link opening Linear directly. Currently only the 'Abrir Linear' button opens Linear — the issue ID and title should also be hyperlinks.

title: Next Action card: add AI-assisted action execution (e.g. create/link PR)
description:
Actions like 'criar ou vincular PR' are not executable from the card. User has no way to act without leaving the notification. Options to explore:
- Inline AI chat that can execute the action (create branch, open PR, link existing PR)
- Quick-action buttons that trigger specific workflows
- At minimum, a 'Start in Claude Code' button that opens a session with context pre-loaded

title: Next Action card: expand card size and show decision-enabling context
description:
Cards are too small to make decisions. Need to show:
- Issue description snippet (first 2-3 lines)
- Current status and sub-status (e.g. UAT, In Review)
- Blockers or dependencies if any
- PR status (draft/open/merged/none)
- Time in current state
Design larger card layout that still fits notification UX but gives enough info to decide next step without opening Linear.

title: Global workflow fallback in br

title: Epic branch strategy + structured handoff between tasks
description:
## Problem
Tasks in an epic execute in separate branches (1 branch per task), never merge to master.
Example: borabr-lw4 — 5 tasks, 5 branches, 4 commits each, 0 merged.
Heavy file overlap (index.vue, useAutoMode.ts, lib.rs) means branches conflict.

## Root cause
No mechanism tells agents to:
1. Share a single branch per epic
2. Pass structured context (branch name, commit SHAs, changed files) between tasks
3. Create a single PR at epic completion

## Solution: workflow JSON + handoff protocol

### A) Branch strategy in workflow JSON
Add to workflow definition:
```json
{
  "branch_strategy": "shared",
  "branch_pattern": "epic/{parent-id}"
}
```
- "shared": all tasks commit to same branch
- "isolated": current behavior (1 branch per task)
- branch_pattern: template for branch name

### B) Structured handoff in step comments
Each step comment includes machine-readable context:
```
step:implement {"commit":"abc123","branch":"epic/borabr-lw4","files":["app/utils/foo.ts"]}
```
Next agent parses this to know: which branch to checkout, what files were touched, what commit to build on.

### C) Epic completion → single PR
When last step closes (or accept gate passes), create ONE PR for the entire epic branch.
Action Center already has the accept/reject gate (Phase 3). Wire it to `gh pr create` from the epic branch.

## Files to modify
- ~/.config/beads/workflows/bug-investigation.json — add branch_strategy
- .beads/workflows/bug-investigation.json — same
- app/utils/workflow-handoff.ts — parse structured JSON from step comments
- Handoff policy text in workflow JSON — update to include branch instructions

## Scope
This is cross-project infrastructure. Changes to global workflow definitions affect ALL projects using br.
close_reason: Added branch_strategy/branch_pattern to workflow JSON (project + global), structured evidence parsing (parseStructuredEvidence, extractBranchName, resolveWorkflowBranchName), branchName/branchStrategy in WorkflowStepHandoff, updated handoff_policy with BRANCH/CONTEXT/EVIDENCE/PR sections. 14 new tests (345 total).

Added tests in tests/utils/workflow-handoff.test.ts. Verified: pnpm test tests/utils/workflow-handoff.test.ts tests/utils/workflow-contracts.test.ts; pnpm exec vue-tsc --noEmit. Remaining: full Action Center approval-to-gh-pr-create wiring is not present in this branch, so leaving task in_progress.

title: CMUX Task Orchestration
description: Use cmux to orchestrate task execution pipeline. For each task: (1) open cmux workspace + git worktree, (2) execute task via dedicated agent, (3) spawn separate review agent for validation/code-review, (4) auto-merge if approved. Pattern: ralph-tui-ary + cnx:cmux integration. Goal: fully parallel, isolated task execution with independent quality gates.
close_reason: Epic complete: all borabr-unf child tasks are closed. Implemented auto-mode polling/dispatch, task-scoped cmux/worktree naming, executor protocol, review gate protocol, approval merge/cleanup protocol, and epic orchestration prompt. Evidence: pnpm test, cargo test, pnpm build for auto-mode trigger; cargo test after orchestration updates; br list shows no open borabr-unf child tasks.

title: cmux workspace creation per task
description: When a task is picked from queue: automatically open a new cmux surface/workspace dedicated to that task. Surface ID stored in BR assignee field for focus tracking.

Child dispatch now uses task:<issue-id> workspace names, returns workspace refs, stores cmux:<workspace-ref> in BR assignee after successful dispatch, and cmux focus/send wrappers handle workspace refs. Evidence: cargo test (40 passed).

title: git worktree lifecycle management
description: For each task: create isolated git worktree branch. On completion/merge: cleanup worktree. Handle conflicts with main. Naming: task-<issue-id>.
close_reason: Implemented task-scoped git worktree lifecycle foundation: child dispatch uses task-<issue-id> branch and worktree names, isolates executor work from main, and orchestrator protocol handles merge success cleanup plus conflict abort/keep-open behavior. Evidence: cargo test auto_mode_.

title: executor agent protocol
description: Define agent spawn protocol: receives issue context, works in isolated worktree, commits atomically, signals completion. Must not touch main branch directly.
close_reason: Implemented executor spawn protocol in Claude dispatch prompt: issue context, task branch/worktree, cmux assignee, in-progress claim, relevant tests, atomic commit, structured completion signal, and no-main-touch constraint. Evidence: cargo test (40 passed).

title: review agent gate (validation + code review)
description: Separate agent spawned post-execution. Runs: tests, type-check, code review against plan. Produces approve/request-changes verdict. Independent context from executor (no bias).
close_reason: Implemented review gate protocol in epic orchestrator dispatch: after executor completion, spawn independent Claude reviewer with fresh context, run tests/type-check/build, review diff against br show, and emit structured approve/request-changes verdict. Evidence: cargo test auto_mode_.

title: auto-merge on approval
description: If review agent approves: merge worktree branch into main, cleanup worktree, close BR issue, sync. If rejected: report findings back, keep worktree open for retry.
close_reason: Implemented auto-merge approval protocol in epic orchestrator dispatch: approved reviews merge task branch into main, abort and keep open on conflicts, remove task worktree, delete branch when safe, close issue, and br sync; rejected reviews keep worktree/issue open with findings. Evidence: cargo test auto_mode_.

title: orchestrator loop (pick → execute → review → merge)
description: Top-level loop: poll br ready, pick highest priority, spawn cmux+worktree+executor, await completion, spawn reviewer, handle verdict. Parallel execution of multiple tasks when no deps.
close_reason: Implemented top-level epic orchestrator dispatch command: poll br ready/open children, pick highest priority, run dependency-aware parallel executor tracks, await executor completion comments, spawn independent reviewers, handle approve/request-changes verdicts, merge/cleanup/close/sync on approval. Evidence: cargo test auto_mode_.

title: auto-mode trigger: poll br ready when enabled, dispatch to cmux+claude
description: Core feature: when auto-mode is ON (per project or branch toggle), app polls br ready. If tasks available and none in_progress → pick highest priority → open cmux workspace + worktree → spawn claude agent to execute. Trigger conditions: (1) app is open, (2) auto-mode enabled, (3) br ready returns items, (4) no task currently in_progress for this project.
close_reason: Implemented auto-mode ready polling, highest-priority non-epic selection, backend in-progress guard, and cmux Claude dispatch; evidence: pnpm test, cargo test, pnpm build

title: Action Center: reconciliar GitHub PRs, Linear e Next Action
description: Objetivo: o Action Center deve ler PRs abertas no GitHub, comparar com tarefas do Linear e com os sinais de Next Action, e propor ações para o usuário não se perder. Escopo inicial: quando uma PR estiver aprovada por alguém, o Action Center deve sugerir mover a tarefa correspondente no Linear para UAT. A análise inversa também faz parte do escopo: para tarefas no Linear, verificar se existe PR/request correspondente, se há comentários relevantes, se a PR está aprovada, bloqueada ou sem resposta, e gerar ações claras. Critérios de aceite: 1. Action Center lista ações vindas de PRs abertas e tarefas Linear correlacionadas. 2. PR aprovada gera ação para mover Linear para UAT quando ainda não estiver nesse status. 3. Tarefa Linear sem PR/request correspondente gera ação de acompanhamento. 4. Tarefa Linear com PR existente e comentários/reviews pendentes gera ação contextual. 5. A reconciliação funciona nos dois sentidos: GitHub para Linear e Linear para GitHub. 6. As ações aparecem em ordem FIFO e com origem identificável para o usuário.
close_reason: Epic completed. Evidence: Action Center now fetches open GitHub PRs for saved projects, scopes them to the authenticated viewer as author or requested reviewer, fetches Linear assigned issues through the cnx/next-action-aligned GraphQL shape, reconciles GitHub<->Linear by PR URL and ticket identifiers, renders Next Action cards with origin, linked PR/ticket, suggested action and FIFO timestamp, and keeps recoverable GitHub/Linear error cards. Acceptance covered by child tasks borabr-vox.4-.7. Dogfood: entrc-backend PR #9865 is excluded because it is not aryrabelo's PR and has no requested reviewers/teams; aryrabelo PRs #12423/#12384 are in scope; cnx next-action Linear returned 4 Ary ENG tickets. Verification: pnpm test, cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml, pnpm build.

Mantendo a epic aberta e criando filhos especificos para o escopo restante.

title: Action Center mostra tarefa pronta em projeto que ja tem trabalho em progresso
description: Bug: o Action Center estava mostrando notificacoes acionaveis de tarefas ready para um projeto que ja tinha uma tarefa em progresso. Isso permite o usuario tentar abrir/rodar duas tarefas ao mesmo tempo na mesma branch, o que quebra o fluxo esperado. Comportamento esperado: se qualquer projeto salvo tiver uma tarefa in_progress, o Action Center nao deve listar outras tarefas ready desse mesmo projeto enquanto o trabalho atual nao sair de in_progress. A notificacao de projeto parado tambem deve respeitar esse estado. Contexto observado: beads_rust tinha uma tarefa in_progress, mas o Action Center ainda mostrava outra tarefa pronta para abrir.
close_reason: Implemented branch-safety guard for Action Center. Ready actions and idle notifications are suppressed when a saved/current project has in_progress work; current project state is reconciled from live issues/readyIssues. Evidence: regression test tests/utils/action-center.test.ts, pnpm test, pnpm build.

title: Reproduce bug
description: Capture the smallest command, fixture, or UI path that reproduces the failure.
acceptance_criteria: The bug is reproduced with concrete evidence before implementation starts.
close_reason: Reproduced with failing regression test: pnpm test tests/utils/action-center.test.ts fails resolving the new Action Center helper before implementation.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: evidence: reproduced before implementation with RED test. Command: pnpm test tests/utils/action-center.test.ts. Result: failed because ~/utils/action-center did not exist yet, proving the regression test was active before the fix.

title: Run five whys
description: Trace the symptom to the smallest root cause and document the causal chain.
acceptance_criteria: A root-cause note explains why the failure happened and why the fix addresses it.
close_reason: Root cause: Action Center cached per-project ready/idle state independently from the live current-project issues. When a task transitioned to in_progress, ready/idle actions could remain visible until the next project polling cycle. Fix centralizes suppression in a pure helper and reconciles current-project Action Center state from live issues/readyIssues.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: evidence: root cause was stale Action Center per-project state. The live issue list could already contain in_progress work while projectActionStates/projectIdleStates still exposed previously fetched ready/idle actions until the next global poll.

title: Verify bug fix
description: Run the focused acceptance test or regression command that proves the bug is fixed.
acceptance_criteria: Verification evidence is recorded before the bug can be completed.
close_reason: Verified: pnpm test passed (22 files, 281 tests); pnpm build passed via nuxt generate. Regression tests cover suppression of ready and idle Action Center entries when a project has in_progress work.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: evidence: implemented app/utils/action-center.ts and wired app/pages/index.vue to suppress ready and idle entries when projectIssues contain in_progress work, including immediate current-project reconciliation from live issues/readyIssues.

title: Action Center: linkar projeto e issue BR
description:
Solicitacao
No Action Center, o nome do projeto deve ser navegavel. Quando uma acao se referir a uma issue do BR, a UI precisa permitir copiar o ID da issue e abrir diretamente essa issue dentro do proprio BoraBR. Caso citado: comunicacao do Action Center para o projeto beads_rust/BeadsRust.

Criterios de aceite
- O nome do projeto em cada item do Action Center funciona como atalho para abrir/trocar para aquele projeto no BoraBR.
- Acoes de issue BR exibem o ID da issue de forma copiavel.
- Acoes de issue BR oferecem link/botao explicito para abrir a issue diretamente dentro do BoraBR.
- O fluxo funciona para itens vindos do projeto beads_rust/BeadsRust e preserva o contexto correto do projeto antes de selecionar a issue.
- Itens que nao sao issue continuam podendo abrir apenas o projeto.
- A UI desktop e mobile/compacta do Action Center ficam consistentes.
close_reason: Implemented Action Center project/issue navigation. Evidence: project names now switch directly to the saved project, issue actions expose CopyableId for the BR ID, primary issue action opens the issue inside BoraBR after switching project context, and desktop/mobile Action Center render paths are consistent. Verification: pnpm test tests/utils/action-center.test.ts, pnpm exec vue-tsc --noEmit, pnpm test, pnpm build.

title: Action Center: copiar prompt de issue

O botão deve copiar para o clipboard o mesmo prompt usado pelo fluxo Rodar no CMUX, para permitir colar manualmente no cmux quando o envio direto falhar. Critérios: cards de issue mostram Copiar prompt; clique usa navigator.clipboard.writeText com feedback; prompt copiado é o mesmo usado por Rodar no CMUX; testes cobrem o builder do prompt.
close_reason: Implemented Copiar prompt button in Action Center issue cards. It copies the same prompt used by Rodar no CMUX via navigator.clipboard.writeText and shows feedback. Evidence: pnpm test tests/utils/action-center.test.ts, pnpm test, pnpm build.

Capturar repo, branch, autor, URL, estado de review/aprovacao, comentarios pendentes e timestamps necessarios para ordenacao FIFO. Criterios: PRs abertas aparecem como sinais normalizados; falha de auth/API nao quebra o Action Center e mostra estado recuperavel; testes cobrem parsing/normalizacao sem rede.

UI now displays GitHub PR/error items. Verification: pnpm test, pnpm exec vue-tsc --noEmit, cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml, pnpm build; dogfood: gh api repos/entrc/entrc-backend/pulls returned open PR payloads.

Criterios: tickets Linear viram sinais normalizados; status UAT e nao-UAT sao distinguiveis; ticket sem PR/request correspondente gera sinal de acompanhamento; testes cobrem mapeamento de payload Linear para sinais do Action Center.

Evidence: Tauri command list_action_center_linear_issues reads LINEAR_API_KEY/~/.linear-credentials, queries viewer.assignedIssues for team BORABR_LINEAR_TEAM/ENG, normalizes identifier/title/url/status/stateType/isUat/assignee/labels/PR URLs/unacked comments/timestamps, caches results, and returns recoverable error payloads. UI now displays Linear issue/error items. Verification: pnpm test, pnpm exec vue-tsc --noEmit, cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml, pnpm build; dogfood: cnx next-action --linear-tasks --json returned 4 ENG tickets.

title: Action Center: reconciliar GitHub, Linear e Next Action
description: Implementar engine de reconciliacao baseado no contrato existente de next-action.ts/Next Action: correlacionar PRs abertas e tickets Linear por branch, URL, external ref, identificador de ticket e metadados. Criterios: PR aprovada sugere mover ticket Linear para UAT quando ainda nao estiver em UAT; Linear sem PR gera acao; Linear com PR e comentarios/reviews pendentes gera acao contextual; reconciliacao funciona GitHub->Linear e Linear->GitHub; origem da acao fica identificavel.
close_reason: Implemented GitHub/Linear/Next Action reconciliation and fixed relevance scope. Evidence: GitHub PR source now resolves authenticated viewer login and only keeps PRs authored by the viewer or explicitly requesting their review, so entrc-backend#9865 (author guimello, no requested reviewers) is excluded. Action Center now renders reconciled Next Action items for move-to-UAT, Linear missing PR, PR attention, Linear comments, and unlinked PR cases. CMUX assignee for the active task was corrected to cmux surface and terminal helper start command now emits a valid cmux assignee command. Verification: pnpm test tests/utils/action-center.test.ts tests/utils/terminal-helpers.test.ts; pnpm exec vue-tsc --noEmit; cargo test action_center_github --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml.

Criterios: cards mostram origem GitHub/Linear/Next Action, PR/ticket vinculados, acao sugerida e timestamp FIFO; PRs abertos do entrc-backend aparecem quando ha acao pendente; tickets Linear que precisam de acao aparecem; dogfood registra evidencia antes de fechar a epic.
close_reason: Validated reconciled Action Center rendering and entrc-backend scope. Evidence: UI now renders Next Action cards from the reconciliation engine with origin label, linked PR/ticket details, suggested action and FIFO timestamp. Dogfood GitHub: entrc-backend has 2 open PRs authored by aryrabelo (#12423, #12384), 0 open PRs requesting aryrabelo review, and #9865 is author guimello with no requested reviewers/teams, so it is excluded. Dogfood Linear via cnx next-action --linear-tasks returned 4 Ary ENG tickets including In Progress without PR and UAT tickets with unread comments. Verification: pnpm test, cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml, pnpm build.

title: Projects sidebar: mostrar worktrees dos projetos salvos
description:
Objetivo
Melhorar a aba Projects da sidebar para que cada projeto salvo mostre tambem suas worktrees relacionadas, nao apenas o diretorio raiz. Exemplo: ao adicionar ~/Sites/entrc-backend, a lista deve mostrar worktrees distintas como ~/worktrees/github.com/entrc/entrc-backend/ary-ENG-732-pt6-conversion-coa-copy quando forem relevantes.

Escopo
- Descobrir worktrees associadas ao projeto salvo.
- Deduplicar worktrees por caminho canonico e branch/repo quando aplicavel.
- Destacar worktrees com PR aberta ou PR mergeada nas ultimas 2 semanas.
- Mostrar tambem as top 5 worktrees mais recentemente alteradas por atividade de arquivos.
- Permitir selecionar uma worktree como contexto de trabalho na UI.

Criterios de aceite
1. A aba Projects continua mostrando o projeto raiz salvo.
2. Worktrees relacionadas aparecem agrupadas abaixo do projeto raiz, sem duplicatas.
3. Worktrees com PR aberta aparecem mesmo que nao estejam entre as mais recentes.
4. Worktrees com PR mergeada nos ultimos 14 dias aparecem com estado/data legivel.
5. As top 5 worktrees por atividade recente aparecem quando nao forem cobertas pelos sinais de PR.
6. O usuario consegue abrir/selecionar a worktree e o restante do app usa esse caminho como contexto do projeto.
close_reason: Epic complete. Evidence: child tasks borabr-vrd.1 through borabr-vrd.5 are closed; discovery/dedupe, GitHub PR promotion, recent activity ranking, Projects sidebar rendering, worktree selection, and validation/dogfood are implemented. Verification included cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml, pnpm test, pnpm exec vue-tsc --noEmit, pnpm build, git diff --check, br dep cycles --json, and dogfood against ~/Sites/entrc-backend with worktrees under ~/worktrees/github.com/entrc/entrc-backend.

title: Descobrir e deduplicar worktrees por projeto salvo
description:
Implementar a camada que encontra worktrees relacionadas a um projeto salvo.

Criterios de aceite
- Para um projeto como ~/Sites/entrc-backend, a app consegue listar worktrees associadas ao mesmo repo, incluindo caminhos em ~/worktrees/github.com/<owner>/<repo> quando existirem.
- A descoberta usa git worktree list --porcelain quando possivel e valida repo/remotes para evitar listar diretorios aleatorios.
- Worktrees sao deduplicadas por caminho canonico e identidade repo+branch quando disponivel.
- Worktrees prunable/inexistentes ou inacessiveis nao quebram a aba Projects.
- A camada retorna um view model estavel com rootPath, worktreePath, branch, head, repoRemote e motivo de inclusao.
close_reason: Implemented worktree discovery layer. Evidence: Tauri command discover_project_worktrees uses git worktree list --porcelain, skips prunable/inaccessible paths, supplements ~/worktrees/github.com/<owner>/<repo> by matching normalized GitHub remotes, dedupes by canonical path and repo+branch, and returns rootPath/worktreePath/canonicalPath/branch/head/repoRemote/isRoot/inclusionReason. Frontend wrapper added as discoverProjectWorktrees. Verification: cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml, pnpm exec vue-tsc --noEmit, pnpm test, pnpm build, and dogfood git worktree list --porcelain.

title: Incluir worktrees com PR aberta ou mergeada nos ultimos 14 dias
description:
Adicionar o sinal de GitHub para promover worktrees relevantes na lista de Projects.

Criterios de aceite
- Para repositorios GitHub, a app identifica a branch de cada worktree e consulta PRs correspondentes.
- Worktrees com PR aberta aparecem sempre, mesmo que nao estejam entre as top 5 recentes.
- Worktrees com PR mergeada nos ultimos 14 dias aparecem com estado merged e data legivel.
- PRs mergeadas ha mais de 14 dias nao entram por esse criterio, mas ainda podem entrar por atividade recente.
- Falha de rede/autenticacao GitHub degrada sem esconder o projeto raiz nem quebrar a sidebar.
- Resultados sao cacheados ou limitados para nao fazer chamadas excessivas ao abrir a sidebar.
close_reason: Implemented GitHub PR promotion signals for project worktrees. Evidence: discover_project_worktrees now detects GitHub owner/repo from remote, queries branch PRs with cached/limited GitHub API calls, marks open PRs and PRs merged in the last 14 days via pullRequest/prPromoted, degrades on API/auth failures without failing discovery, and excludes PR-promoted paths from recent ranks. Added Rust tests for open PR preference, recent merged PR inclusion, and old merged PR exclusion. Verification: cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml and pnpm exec vue-tsc --noEmit.

title: Rankear top 5 worktrees por atividade recente de arquivos
description:
Definir e implementar o ranking local das worktrees mais recentemente alteradas.

Criterios de aceite
- A app calcula lastActivityAt por worktree usando o mtime mais recente de arquivos de trabalho relevantes.
- A varredura ignora .git, node_modules, dist, build, target, .next, .nuxt e outros diretorios gerados pesados.
- O algoritmo tem limite de tempo/quantidade para nao travar a UI em repos grandes.
- Quando nao houver arquivos elegiveis, faz fallback para data do HEAD ou metadado Git disponivel.
- A aba Projects inclui no maximo 5 worktrees por atividade recente, alem das promovidas por PR.
- Worktrees ja incluidas por PR nao aparecem duplicadas no bloco de recentes.
close_reason: Implemented recent activity ranking for project worktrees. Evidence: discover_project_worktrees now returns lastActivityAt/lastActivitySource/activityScanLimited/recentActivityRank, scans relevant file mtimes with generated-dir and .git exclusions plus entry/time limits, falls back to Git HEAD or metadata, ranks the top 5 non-root recent worktrees, and supports excluding PR-promoted worktrees from recent ranks. Added Rust tests for ignored generated dirs, .git file exclusion, and top-5/promoted ranking. Verification: cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml, pnpm exec vue-tsc --noEmit, pnpm test, pnpm build, git diff --check.

title: Renderizar worktrees agrupadas na aba Projects
description:
Atualizar a experiencia visual da aba Projects para exibir worktrees relacionadas abaixo do projeto raiz.

Criterios de aceite
- Cada projeto salvo renderiza o diretorio raiz e, abaixo dele, uma lista de worktrees distintas.
- Worktrees mostram nome/branch legivel, caminho curto, badges para PR open, merged recente e recent activity quando aplicavel.
- Selecionar uma worktree troca o contexto do app para o caminho da worktree, preservando o projeto raiz como grupo.
- Estado expandido/colapsado e selecao sao previsiveis e nao causam layout shift.
- A UI permanece usavel quando ha zero, poucas ou muitas worktrees.
close_reason: Implemented Projects sidebar worktree rendering. Evidence: PathSelector loads discoverProjectWorktrees per saved project, renders promoted/recent worktrees grouped under each project root with stable expanded state, PR/recent/limited badges, and selects a worktree by setting beadsPath to its canonical path. Header now falls back to the selected path folder name for worktree contexts. Helper tests cover visible worktree filtering, badge generation, display names, and short paths. Verification: pnpm exec vitest run tests/utils/favorites-helpers.test.ts, pnpm exec vue-tsc --noEmit, pnpm build.

title: Validar fluxo Projects + worktrees com testes e dogfood
description:
Cobrir o fluxo de ponta a ponta antes de fechar a epic.

Criterios de aceite
- Testes unitarios cobrem parse de git worktree list --porcelain, deduplicacao e exclusao de worktrees invalidas.
- Testes cobrem inclusao por PR aberta, PR mergeada nos ultimos 14 dias e exclusao de PR mergeada fora da janela.
- Testes cobrem ranking top 5 por lastActivityAt e deduplicacao entre PR/recentes.
- Testes/componentes cobrem renderizacao da sidebar Projects com root + worktrees e troca de contexto.
- Dogfood documenta um projeto real com worktrees em ~/worktrees/github.com/... e registra evidencia na issue antes de fechar.
close_reason: Validated Projects + worktrees flow. Evidence recorded in comment 25: Rust tests, Vitest suite, vue-tsc, Nuxt build, diff whitespace check, dependency cycle check, and dogfood against ~/Sites/entrc-backend with worktrees under ~/worktrees/github.com/entrc/entrc-backend.

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Validation evidence 2026-05-01: cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml passed (31 Rust tests); pnpm test passed (22 files, 288 tests); pnpm exec vue-tsc --noEmit passed; pnpm build passed with existing duplicate-import/dynamic-import warnings; git diff --check passed; br dep cycles --json returned count 0. Dogfood: ~/Sites/entrc-backend exists, remote is git@github.com:entrc/entrc-backend.git, git worktree list --porcelain includes ~/worktrees/github.com/entrc/entrc-backend/ary-ENG-732-pt4-source-coa-fallback, ary-ENG-732-pt5-sibling-coa, ary-ENG-732-pt6-conversion-coa-copy, CNX-1007, CNX-1209, and more; directory scan of ~/worktrees/github.com/entrc/entrc-backend also found those worktree folders.

title: Rebrand and publish fork as BoraBR
description:
Goal
Reposition this project as Ary's personal fork named BoraBR, published under the personal GitHub account and ready for continued development.

Scope
- Preserve the upstream project history and keep a reference to the original remote.
- Create or use aryrabelo/BoraBR as the personal GitHub repository.
- Rename visible project metadata from Beads Task-Issue Tracker to BoraBR where appropriate.
- Keep technical compatibility with br/bd behavior unless intentionally changed later.

Implementation Plan
1. Set up GitHub remotes so origin points to aryrabelo/BoraBR and upstream points to w3dev33/beads-task-issue-tracker.
2. Update product/package/Tauri metadata and user-facing text to use BoraBR.
3. Update docs and onboarding copy for the fork identity and current br-first workflow.
4. Run dev, tests, typecheck, and production Tauri build.
5. Commit and push the rebrand work to the personal repo.

Acceptance Criteria
- Repo exists at github.com/aryrabelo/BoraBR with the current history pushed.
- Local remotes are unambiguous: personal repo for pushes, original repo preserved as upstream.
- App name and docs consistently present BoraBR without breaking build metadata.
- Validation evidence is attached in comments before closing: pnpm test, npx vue-tsc --noEmit, and pnpm exec tauri build.
close_reason: Epic complete: repo remotes, metadata rebrand, docs updates, and validation were finished in prior cycles (commits 729f6c3 and c303a94), including pnpm test, vue-tsc --noEmit, cargo test, pnpm build, and tauri build.

title: Create personal GitHub repo and configure remotes
description:
Set up aryrabelo/BoraBR as the personal repository for this fork.

Acceptance Criteria
- GitHub repository aryrabelo/BoraBR exists.
- origin points to the personal repository for push/fetch.
- upstream points to w3dev33/beads-task-issue-tracker.
- Current branch can push to the personal repository.
close_reason: Created public GitHub repository aryrabelo/BoraBR, configured origin to git@github.com:aryrabelo/BoraBR.git, preserved upstream as git@github.com:w3dev33/beads-task-issue-tracker.git, and pushed master successfully.

title: Rename app and package metadata to BoraBR
description:
Update user-visible and package metadata for the new identity.

Acceptance Criteria
- package metadata uses BoraBR where appropriate.
- Tauri product name/window/bundle metadata use BoraBR where appropriate.
- In-app About/header/title text no longer presents the fork as the original product except where upstream attribution is intentional.
- Version/build metadata remains coherent.
close_reason: Implemented BoraBR rebrand in package/Tauri/app metadata and UI strings, added metadata tests, updated updater URLs and version to 1.42.0. Validation: pnpm test, vue-tsc, cargo test, pnpm build, tauri build.

title: Update docs and agent instructions for BoraBR
description:
Refresh repo documentation for the fork.

Acceptance Criteria
- README introduces BoraBR and preserves upstream attribution.
- Build-from-source and development commands match the actual local workflow.
- Agent instructions prefer br when bd is unavailable or the project-local .beads workspace is used.
- Any old repo/release references are either updated or explicitly documented as upstream history.
close_reason: Updated README and AGENTS.md for BoraBR identity with new clone/build/release/app update references and br-first agent instructions, while preserving upstream context and historical compatibility notes.

title: Validate BoraBR dev and production builds
description:
Run the quality gates after the rebrand work.

Acceptance Criteria
- pnpm test passes.
- npx vue-tsc --noEmit passes.
- pnpm exec tauri build passes and emits macOS artifacts.
- Development mode opens successfully through pnpm exec tauri dev or a documented equivalent.
- Validation output is summarized in a comment on the epic before closure.
close_reason: Validation completed as part of rebrand cycle: pnpm test, vue-tsc --noEmit, cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml, pnpm build, pnpm exec tauri build, and release artifacts were produced (BoraBR.app / BoraBR_1.42.0_aarch64.dmg).
